analysis.consistency: The spell description is internally consistent regarding its mechanics (casting time, range, components, duration, and general function). The bargaining/payment structure follows a clear, albeit suggested, guideline (gp per minute/hour/day), which aids consistency in gameplay application.
analysis.quality: This is a high-quality spell description common to 5th Edition D&D. It clearly defines the primary function (summoning an entity for service), the critical limitation (no inherent compulsion, requiring bargaining), and provides concrete advice for the Game Master on determining payment scales. The narrative flavor is strong.

key_mechanical_points: Summons a celestial, elemental, or fiend loyal to a known powerful entity.
The summoned creature is not compelled to obey; negotiation for service is required.
Payment structure is provided as a baseline: 100 gp/min, 1,000 gp/hour, 10,000 gp/day (up to 10 days).
GM has discretion to adjust payment based on task alignment, danger, and circumstances.
The creature returns upon task completion or agreed duration expiry.
The creature counts as a party member for XP sharing.
mechanics.class_suitability: Appropriate for Clerics (Level 6), fitting the theme of calling upon divine or powerful external support.
